title: Teacher — Assignments
content:
Overview


The Assignments page allows teachers to create and send assignments to students enrolled in their subjects. All sent assignments are listed for review. Students receive assignments on their Student Portal Assignments page.



Creating an Assignment


  1. Click New Assignment at the top of the Assignments page
  2. Select the Subject from the dropdown — only subjects the teacher has created appear here
  3. Enter the Title (e.g., "Unit 3 Practice" or "Chapter 5 Review")
  4. Enter the Description — this is where the teacher provides instructions. The description field supports detailed text so teachers can include specific directions like "Complete Unit 3 Quizzes 1 to 5" or "Study the past questions because your test will be similar"
  5. Review the student count shown below the description — this confirms how many enrolled students will receive the assignment
  6. Click Send Assignment

The assignment is immediately delivered to all students enrolled in the selected subject. They will see it on their Assignments page the next time they log in.



Viewing Sent Assignments


The Sent Assignments section lists all assignments the teacher has created. Each assignment card shows:


  • Title of the assignment
  • Subject it belongs to
  • Date sent
  • Student count — how many students received it

If no assignments have been sent yet, an empty state message is displayed: "No assignments sent yet."



How Students Receive Assignments


Students see assignments on the Assignments page in their Student Portal. They can view the title, subject, teacher name, description, and date sent. Assignments are read-only for students — they cannot submit work through the portal. The assignment serves as a task list guiding students on what to study or practice.


Parents can also view assignments sent to their children through the Assignments page in the Parent Portal.



Key Talking Points for Agents


  • "Assignments go to all students enrolled in the selected subject. The student count confirms how many will receive it."
  • "Use the description field to give detailed instructions — students will see exactly what you write."
  • "Students can't submit work through the portal. The assignment is a study guide telling them what to work on."
  • "Assignments are delivered instantly. Students will see them the next time they log in."
  • "You can see all your past assignments in the Sent Assignments section."
